title: Electrical terminal
abstract: An electrical terminal includes a contact partially surrounded by a hood. In an embodiment, the contact has a deflectable contact beam and an indentation with which a male terminal engages. In an embodiment, the hood has a polarization rib having at least one flap which is configured to expand the seal. The electrical terminal is used with a connector assembly having a housing forward of a seal and a seal cover rearward thereof. A first length is defined between ends of the portion of the hood that surrounds the contact and a second length is defined between the ends of the seal. In an embodiment, the first length is a minimum of 1.1 times the second length.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. An electrical terminal comprising: a contact formed of a body having opposite front and rear ends, a passageway formed therethrough, and deflectable contact beam extending from a wall of the body and into the passageway; and a hood partially surrounding the contact, the hood formed of a body having opposite front and rear ends, a passageway formed through the body of the hood, a planar top wall, and a polarization rib extending from the top wall and overlapping a portion of an upper surface of the top wall, wherein a front end of the polarization rib is spaced rearwardly from the front end of the body of the hood, and a front flap extends from the front end of the polarization rib and at an angle relative to the planar top wall, wherein a free end of the front flap is proximate to the top wall. 2. The electrical terminal of claim 1, wherein a rear end of the polarization rib is spaced rearwardly from the rear end of the body of the hood, and further comprising a rear flap extending from a rear end of the polarization rib and at an angle relative to the planar top wall, wherein a free end of the rear flap is proximate to the top wall. 3. The electrical terminal of claim 2, further comprising curved flanges extending from the front end of the body of the hood. 4. The electrical terminal of claim 2, further comprising a retaining finger extending at an angle relative to the rear end of the hood, the retaining finger partially obstructing a rear end of the passageway through the contact. 5. The electrical terminal of claim 1, further comprising a lock projection extending upward from the top wall, and wherein the polarization rib has a lock recess provided in a lower surface thereof, the lock projection seating within the lock recess. 6. The electrical terminal of claim 5, further comprising a second lock projection extending upward from the top wall, and wherein the polarization rib has a second lock recess provided in a lower surface thereof, the second lock projection seating within the second lock recess. 7. The electrical terminal of claim 1, wherein the polarization rib has a front section and a rear section separated by a space, wherein the front flap extends from a front end of the front section, and further comprising a front flap extending from a front end of the rear section which is proximate to the space, the front flap extending from the rear section extending at angle relative to the planar top wall, wherein an end of the front flap extending from the rear section within the space. 8. The electrical terminal of claim 7, further comprising an opening through the top wall, the opening at least partially aligning with the space. 9. The electrical terminal of claim 7, wherein a rear end of the rear section of the polarization rib is spaced rearwardly from the rear end of the body of the hood, and further comprising a rear flap extending from a rear end of the rear section and at an angle relative to the planar top wall, wherein a free end of the rear flap is proximate to the top wall. 10. The electrical terminal of claim 9, further comprising a retaining finger extending at an angle relative to the rear end of the hood, the retaining finger partially obstructing a rear end of the passageway through the contact. 11. The electrical terminal of claim 7, further comprising a front lock projection extending upward from the top wall, and wherein the front section of the polarization rib has a front lock recess provided in a lower surface thereof, the front lock projection seating within the front lock recess, and a rear lock projection extending upward from the top wall, and wherein the rear section of the polarization rib has a rear lock recess provided in a lower surface thereof, the rear lock projection seating within the rear lock recess. 12. The electrical terminal of claim 1, further comprising curved flanges extending from the front end of the body of the hood. 13. The electrical terminal of claim 1, wherein the contact further comprises a wire securing portion configured to connect to exposed conductors of a lead wire and to connect to insulation of the lead wire. 14. The electrical terminal of claim 13 in combination with a connector assembly comprising a housing, a seal and a seal cover, the housing being disposed forward of the seal and the seal cover being disposed rearward of the seal, wherein a first length is defined by a portion of the hood that overlays the contact, and a second length is defined between front and rear ends of the seal, the first length being a minimum of 1.1 times the second length. 15. The combination of claim 14, wherein a third length is defined by a gap formed by the seal between the seal cover and the housing, and a fourth length is defined between the front and rear ends of the hood, wherein the third length is less than the fourth length. 16. The electrical terminal of claim 1, further comprising an indentation formed in a wall of the body of the contact opposite from the wall from which the deflectable contact beam extends, the indentation extending into the passageway of the contact, the deflectable contact beam and the indentation aligning with each other. 17. The electrical terminal of claim 16, further comprising a deflectable stiffening beam extending from a wall of the hood, wherein the contact beam is configured to contact the stiffening beam. 18. The electrical terminal of claim 16 in combination with a male terminal, the male terminal being configured to engage with the indentation and the contact beam upon insertion into the contact. 19. An electrical terminal comprising: a contact formed of a body having opposite front and rear ends, a passageway formed therethrough, a deflectable contact beam extending from a first wall of the body and into the passageway, and an indentation formed in a second wall of the body and into the passageway, the second wall being opposite from the wall from which the deflectable contact beam extends; and a hood partially surrounding the contact, the hood formed of a body having opposite front and rear ends, a passageway formed through the body of the hood. 18 20. The electrical terminal of claim 19 in combination with a male terminal, the male terminal being configured to engage with the indentation and the contact beam upon insertion into the contact. 21. The electrical terminal of claim 19, wherein the indentation includes an angled portion which transitions into a bowl-like portion. 22. The electrical terminal of claim 19, further comprising a deflectable stiffening beam extending from a wall of the hood, wherein the contact beam is configured to contact the stiffening beam. 23. The electrical terminal of claim 22 in combination with a male terminal, the male terminal being configured to engage with the indentation and the contact beam upon insertion into the contact. 24. The electrical terminal of claim 19, wherein the hood further comprises a planar top wall, and a polarization rib extending from the top wall and overlapping a portion of an upper surface of the top wall. 25. The electrical terminal of claim 24, further comprising at least one of front flap extending from an end of the polarization rib and at an angle relative to the planar top wall and having a free end is proximate to the top wall. 26. The electrical terminal of claim 24, further comprising a front flap extending from a front end of the polarization rib and at an angle relative to the planar top wall, a free end of the front flap being proximate to the top wall, and a rear flap extending from a rear end of the polarization rib and at an angle relative to the planar top wall, wherein a free end of the rear flap is proximate to the top wall. 27. A combination comprising: an electrical terminal comprising a contact formed of a body having opposite front and rear ends, a passageway formed therethrough, a deflectable contact beam extending from the body and into the 19 

passageway, and a wire securing portion configured to connect to exposed conductors of a lead wire and to connect to insulation of the lead wire, and a hood partially surrounding a portion of the contact, the hood formed of a body having opposite front and rear ends, a passageway formed through the body of the hood, a planar top wall, and a polarization rib extending from the top wall and overlapping a portion of an upper surface of the top wall, wherein a first length is defined by a portion of the hood that overlays the contact; and a connector assembly comprising a housing, a seal and a seal cover, the housing being disposed forward of the seal and the seal cover being disposed rearward of the seal, wherein a second length is defined between front and rear ends of the seal, the first length being a minimum of 1.1 times the second length. 28. The combination of claim 27, wherein a third length is defined by a gap formed by the seal between the seal cover and the housing, and a fourth length is defined between the front and rear ends of the hood, wherein the third length is less than the fourth length. 29. The combination of claim 27, further comprising a deflectable stiffening beam extending from a wall of the hood, wherein the contact beam is configured to contact the stiffening beam. 30. The combination of claim 29, wherein the contact includes an indentation formed in a wall of the body opposite to the deflectable contact beam, the indentation extending into the passageway; and further comprising a male terminal, the male terminal being configured to engage with the indentation and the contact beam upon insertion into the contact. 20
